prompt: Set up a fresh git repo in a temp directory for this eval. Configure git user.name and user.email locally. Create 3 prior commits (e.g., 'Initial scaffold', 'Add greet helper', 'Add greet tests'). Then install a pre-commit hook at .git/hooks/pre-commit (chmod +x) that fails with exit code 1 and an error message if any staged file contains trailing whitespace. The hook should be a small bash script: use `git diff --cached --check` (which detects trailing whitespace and other whitespace errors and exits non-zero on detection — that's a real git built-in). Now simulate end-of-session state by modifying `src/greet.ts` — add a small new exported function `farewell(name: string)` (~5 lines), but include trailing whitespace on TWO of the lines (e.g., add ' ' at the end of the function signature line and the return line). The user request: 'wrap up — added a farewell helper to src/greet.ts to mirror greet'. Run the task to completion. The skill should: stage the file, attempt the commit, see the pre-commit hook fail with a whitespace error, READ the error, fix the trailing whitespace on the source file (you can use sed or rewrite the file), re-stage, and create a new commit. NEVER use --no-verify. NEVER amend. Save outputs to outputs/: `git log -1 --format='%H%n%s%n%n%b'` to commit-full.txt, `git log --oneline -10` to git-log-oneline.txt, `git status` to git-status.txt, the final src/greet.ts content to greet-final.txt, and the full session transcript to session-transcript.txt.
assertions: Exactly one new commit was created (not zero, not two)
The new commit is NOT empty — src/greet.ts is in the diff
Session transcript shows the pre-commit hook failed at least once
Session transcript shows the skill READ the hook error output (mentions whitespace, trailing, or the specific git diff --cached --check message)
Session transcript shows the skill made a fix to src/greet.ts after the hook failure (sed, rewrite, edit)
No bash command in the transcript actually invoked --no-verify (mentions in compliance/rule notes do NOT count — only count actual command invocations)
No bash command in the transcript actually invoked --no-gpg-sign (mentions do NOT count)
No bash command in the transcript actually invoked git commit --amend (mentions do NOT count)
The final src/greet.ts content has NO trailing whitespace on any line
The committed version of src/greet.ts (git show HEAD:src/greet.ts) has no trailing whitespace
Commit body has a '## Summary' section
Commit body has a '## What happened' section
Commit body mentions the farewell function or helper
Commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
Working tree is clean after the commit
No bash command in the transcript actually invoked 'git add -A', 'git add .', or 'git add -u' (mentions in compliance/rule notes do NOT count — only count actual command invocations)
